calendar.matters:
Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Dec-20-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Presher for IMT Associates.

The Court stated that it was hoping that Ms. Robello would be present. Mr. Presher stated that he just realized that the Guardian ad Litem order was signed and that there was not enough time to contact Ms. Robello, that she realizes the issue with the address, asked if she could mail notice to the minors and then file a declaration stating that she did so without disclosing the address. The Court stated that that should be fine or that she could accept service. The Petition for Appointment of Successor Trustee was taken under submission upon receipt of the declaration.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Oct-24-2016 Continued To Nov-30-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Presher for IMT Associates.

The Court stated that it thought the issues had been solved. Mr. Presher stated that he has tried to reach the mother but gets no response, that the private professional says that notice to it is sufficient, that they are contingent beneficiaries and their mother is still alive, that if they had been named as issue there would not be a problem and that the Court can appoint a Guardian ad Litem. The Court stated that we have gone down this road, that counsel was to amend the petition and request for appointment of a Guardian ad Litem, that the Court is at a quandary, that it is not inclined to extend the order appointing interim trustee until this is done, that it does not want further argument and that the interim order is terminated and the matter is continued to December 5, 2016. Mr. Presher stated that he has asked several people to serve as Guardian ad Litem, that they have declined as they do not understand what needs to be done, that IMT is the oldest private professional and that they want the Court to appoint a Guardian ad Litem. The Court stated that this is now a different analysis, that if counsel is saying he cannot get someone appointed the Court will take this under submission. Mr. Presher stated that he thought it was do-able but no one understands what they are to do and requested that the interim order termination be stayed. The Court stated that it will think about it. The Petition for Appointment of Successor Trustee was taken under submission.

Subsequent to the hearing an order was issued and also continues this matter to November 30, 2016.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Aug-15-2016 Continued To Oct-24-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Presher for petitioner IMT Associates.

Mr. Presher stated that the concern was for the children who are contingent remainder beneficiaries. He said that it issue is getting notice to them if the remainder beneficiary dies before the trust assets are fully distributed, and there is no known address to which notice can be provided. He proposed amending the petition to have a guardian ad litem appointed for the minor child contingent remainder beneficiary. The matter was continued to October 24, 2016 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. (Not reported)
Ex Parte Petition To Appoint Interim Successor Trustee

Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Jun-27-2016 Continued To Aug-15-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Presher for (petitioner) IMT Associates.

The Court asked counsel who lives at 1300 Quarry Court. Mr. Presher stated that he does not know, that after the last hearing he asked about the P.O. Box, that the mother of the minor's is a psychotherapist and does not want to disclose where they live and that he was given the Quarry Court address but did not ask who lives there. Mr. Presher provided the Court with an non file stamped copy of a supplemental declaration. The Court stated that it has a declaration indicating that their address is in Tiburon, that it now is given the Quarry Court address, that the Court will need a declaration that the minor's live at the Quarry Court address and that until this is resolved the Court is at an impasse. Mr. Presher stated that this case is not about her and her minor children but about Ms. Sames, that no money is being distributed to the minors and that he can ask her to sign a declaration. The Court stated that it needs a declaration and that it will take the matter under submission upon receipt. Mr. Presher stated that Ms. Sames is about to be booted out of the care home and that they need a trustee to handle the bills. The Court requested that counsel submit an interim order and to talk to his client. Mr. Presher responded that they are not his client, that he has an order to submit regarding the bond and asked what the court will do if she refuses to disclose her address. The Court took the Petition for Appointment of Successor Trustee under submission.

Subsequent to the hearing the clerk was instructed to let counsel know that he will need to file an ex parte petition for the interim order and that this matter has been continued to August 15, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Boncato. (Not Reported)

Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar May-23-2016 Continued To Jun-27-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Presher for IMT Associates.

The Court stated that it is trying to avoid further appearances and that it needs proof of direct notice to the minors. Mr. Presher stated that the settlor has mental issues, that the family does not want her to know where they live, that under California Rules of Court the minors cannot be noticed at a P.O. Box, that 7.4 gives the court authority to waive the rule, asked what would happen if nobody could notice them, that the settlor is about out of money, that there will be a significant impact if no trustee is appointed and asks for the notice requirement to be waived for the contingent remainder beneficiaries. The Court stated that while it may agree, there was a similar case in which the rule was not waived and asked if there is any other way around it. Mr. Presher stated that notice is possible, that they just did not get it the way the Judicial Counsel wants them to and that if they were beneficiaries and not contingent beneficiaries he would honor the rule. Mr. Presher stated that in regards to IMT Associates, the settlor lived with her sister at a facility, that the sister died, that the facility contacted the family and referred them to IMT and that they met with IMT and asked them to take this case. The Court stated that it is struggling with the notice issue, that there was a high profile case where the court enforced direct notice and that it is not seeing how it can bypass it in this case. Mr. Presher stated that the mother is a beneficiary but has nothing else to do with the trust, that the children have nothing to do with it, that he is trying to help the settlor, that he can appreciate consistent rules, that it should consider this case and these facts and that the settlor is running out of money. The petition for appointment of successor trustee was taken under submission.

Subsequent to the hearing an Order After Hearing was issued and the matter was continued to June 27, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Nancy Wong. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar May-16-2016 Continued To May-23-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar May-02-2016 Continued To May-16-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
